no_trade_root_causes: 53.3% of days have no trade — primary cause is FirstHourVerdict requiring FH move > 0.3% which only triggers ~15% of days
BB Squeeze (39% of all trades) occupies the 12:15-14:15 slot but loses money — it is blocking other strategies from taking that slot on good days
Journal morning_scan fields are not being populated (regime/gap_type show as null) — this prevents regime-based filtering and diagnostics

first_hour_verdict.finding: Best strategy by far. 68% WR, 1.79 R:R, Rs 2.4M PnL. This is the anchor of the system.

bb_squeeze.finding: 38.2% WR when breakeven requires 48.2%. Loses on every day of the week. This strategy is taking 455 trades (63% of all trades) and producing -Rs 449K. Disabling it would improve overall performance AND free up trade slots for better strategies.

gap_fill.finding: Good win rate, small sample. Keep but needs more opportunity.

gap_up_fade.status: KEEP — too few trades to judge, but positive
gap_up_fade.finding: Only 2 trades — needs large gap-up days (>0.75%) which are rare. Lower threshold to 0.5%.

vwap_reversion.finding: Strategy not firing due to RSI defaulting to 50 (data access bug — fixed in code). When enabled with corrected RSI, R:R is inverted: risking 0.5% stop to target 0.3% VWAP reversion. Fix: stop = 0.25% tight stop, target = VWAP + extension to get 1:1.5 minimum R:R. Then re-enable with RSI 70/30 thresholds.

best_performing_conditions: First hour with strong directional move (>0.3%) — 68% WR regardless of day
Small gap-down (0.1-0.5%) on Monday/Friday — gap fill works well
Friday first-hour long trades benefit from bullish filter (1.3x multiplier)
US bearish sentiment (S&P < -0.5%) + gap-down setup — trend continuation works
worst_performing_conditions: BB Squeeze on ANY day — 38% WR across all regimes and days of week
BB Squeeze on Monday (34.8% WR, -Rs 124K) and Friday (34.9% WR, -Rs 178K) are worst
Tuesday BB Squeeze is least bad (44.2%) but still below breakeven
Days with no first-hour move and no gap — no strategy has edge

filter_threshold_recommendation.reason: Current threshold is correct. The real problem is not the filter stack — it's that bb_squeeze is taking trades it should not. Disabling bb_squeeze (weight=0.0) is the right fix, not loosening filters.

key_learnings: FirstHourVerdict is the only strategy with proven edge (68% WR, 1.79 R:R). Build around it.
BB Squeeze needs R:R and WR improvements before re-enabling. The intraday 15m period=10 squeeze threshold is likely catching noise, not genuine squeezes. Consider multi-day BB percentile instead.
53% no-trade days is acceptable if the trading days are highly profitable. With FH alone: 225 trades at 68% WR is better than 715 trades at 49% WR.
Journal morning_scan context fields are empty — fix the logger to capture regime/gap_type from the briefing so future analysis can segment by these conditions.
Global pre-market data (VIX, S&P) is now captured but not yet used in scoring. High VIX (>20) days should reduce position size by 50%. Bearish US overnight should boost short signals.
GapUpFade is hitting 100% but only on 2 trades. Lower the min_gap_up_pct from 0.75% to 0.5% to capture more opportunities.
action_items: IMMEDIATE: Set bb_squeeze scorer weight to 0.0 (effectively disabled until redesigned)
IMMEDIATE: Fix journal logger to write morning_scan.regime and morning_scan.gap_type from the briefing dict
NEXT: Redesign bb_squeeze — use multi-day (20-day lookback) BB percentile not intraday-only; require ADX < 20 for genuine range day
NEXT: Fix VWAPReversion R:R — tighten stop to 0.25% and extend target to VWAP + (entry-VWAP)*0.5 for 1:1.5 R:R
NEXT: Lower GapUpFade min_gap_up_pct from 0.75 to 0.5 to get more opportunities
FUTURE: Integrate India VIX into filter stack — when VIX > 20, multiply all position sizes by 0.7
FUTURE: Use S&P overnight change as pre-market filter — bearish US should boost short_multiplier by 1.1
